PLGA  NPs  are  stable  that  means  passed  stability  test  upto  6  moths  storage  and
the phosphate buffer containing pH-7.4 used for this study at 4 °C. Therefore, P
80 MNG PLGA NPs showed a great stability for the storage upto 6 months based
on ZP, Particle Size, PDI, and EE (%) observations. We got the acceptable results
for size of NPs after the 6 months storage that means a very little enhancement of
opt-NPs size. In this study, maximum size of NPs was 131.43 ± 3.27 nm after the
6 moths storage that means it is an acceptable range. Ahmad et al. was reported
the before there was a very little alteration in size of particles for CS TA PLGA
[17]. The optimized MNG PLGA NPs and P 80 MNG PLGA NPs nanoformula-
tions were showed larger particle sizes (103.4 ± 2.66 and 131.43 ± 3.27 nm) due
to the entrapment of the MNG inside the PLGA NPs core, and coated with P 80.
The excellent delivery to brain in terms of NPs having the NPs less than 100 nm
was  reported  earlier  [59],  but  on  the  other  case  they  reported < 200  nm  can  be
easily  crossed  the  NPs  to  brain  from  BBB  [17,  36].  Based  on  ZP  study  evalua-
tion for stability, −10.0 mV to + 10.0 mV: Neutral; < −30.0 mV & >  + 30.0 mV:
higher stability of NPs [17, 36]. Higher ZP, i.e., −35.0 mV to + 35.0 mV showed
maximum repulsions electrically to increase the stability of NPs and maintained
the segregation of NPs. PDI is used as a determination of distribution factor for
opt-NPs which should be lower than ≤ 0.5 that means maximum numbers of NPs
are in same size [17, 36]. We also got the PDI results < 0.5 for P80 MNG PLGA
NPs after their coating and loading of MNG.
